prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7e1b90c2d48f5","BT":2,"BW":3,"HF":0.38,"RF":0.27,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.15,"behavioral_consistency":0.34,"response_latency_variance":7.22,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.45,"error_recovery_pattern":0.065,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.71,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.28,"gas_price_precision":0.61,"rationale":"Simulates a casual DeFi user with concentrated activity hours (low entropy), irregular timing (high latency variance), simple tx patterns, and imprecise gas settings — a low-engagement human profile at roughly -1.3σ on hour_entropy and eip1559_tip, +1.3σ on behavioral_consistency and latency_variance."}
